steps.title: Map the passage structure
steps.content: The passage identifies an unusual feature of the Aubrey/Maturin novels: their endings can seem abrupt. It then gives one critical view of that feature and a contrasting view that treats the series as one long work.

steps.title: Choose the two-viewpoint purpose
steps.content: Choice A adds entertainment, which is not discussed. Choice B claims Treason's Harbour is one of O'Brian's most complex books, which the passage never says. Choice D turns the comparison to Proust into an argument for literary renown. The remaining choice describes a characteristic and two viewpoints about it. The answer is C.
